{ "paper_id": "pa-02-foundation", "source_file": "\\dlowenas\brain\axioms\00_INBOX_DROP_PAPERS_HERE\pa-02-foundation.md", "generated_at": "2026-05-11T18:08:45", "metrics": { "word_count": 304, "section_count": 20, "equation_count": 1, "claim_candidate_count": 6, "top_terms": "substrate, information, node, because, primitive, distinction, self-grounding, derivation, existence, definition, dead-ends, badge, html, axiom, rather" }, "sections": [ { "title": "Document Start", "text": "::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.main role="main"}
::::::::: hero
:::: {}
::: kicker" }, { "title": "Section 1", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "Foundation Spine", "text": "The foundational chain: existence, distinction, information primacy, substrate, self-grounding, and axiomatic generation.
::::
:::::: badge-row
::: badge" }, { "title": "Self-contained HTML", "text": ":::
::: badge" }, { "title": "Interlinked pages", "text": ":::
::: badge" }, { "title": "Academic presentation", "text": ":::
::::::
:::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::: {.grid .two}
::::::::::::::: {}
:::: {.node .axiom}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Axiom", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "A1.1 Existence", "text": "Something exists rather than nothing. Denial is self-refuting because denial itself presupposes a real act.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Derivation", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "A1.2 Distinction", "text": "If anything exists, it must be distinguishable from what it is not. Existence without distinction collapses into undifferentiated nullity.
::::
:::: {.node .definition}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Definition", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "A1.3 Information Primacy", "text": "Distinguishability is informational. Information is treated as primitive because every rival primitive borrows distinction-language to describe itself.
::::
:::: {.node .definition}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Definition", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "A2.1 Substrate Requirement", "text": "Information cannot float as a free abstraction. It requires a real substrate in which distinctions are borne.
::::
:::: {.node .commitment}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Commitment", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "A2.2 Self-Grounding", "text": "The substrate must not regress infinitely. The framework marks self-grounding as an explicit commitment rather than a secretly smuggled derivation.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation}
::: tag" }, { "title": "Derivation", "text": ":::" }, { "title": "Logos Field → 189 Axioms", "text": "The self-grounding substrate is formalized as the Logos Field, then unfolded into the axiomatic library that generates the rest of the proof surface.
::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::: {}
::: trace" }, { "title": "Alternative primitive eliminations", "text": "**Matter primitive:** dead-ends because matter is only specified by properties, and properties are informational distinctions.
**Math primitive:** dead-ends because abstract relations describe but do not instantiate a causal substrate.
**Consciousness primitive:** dead-ends because consciousness itself requires an account of distinction, state, and substrate.
**Emergent information:** dead-ends because emergence presupposes a more basic structure to emerge from.
:::" }, { "title": "Formal compression", "text": "::: eq
Existence → Distinction → Information → Substrate → Self-Grounding → Logos Field → Axiom System
:::
::: callout
This page is the entry gate. If the foundation fails, everything downstream fails. If it stands, the rest of the proof becomes a propagation problem rather than a vague metaphysical assertion.
:::
::::::
::::::::::::::::::::
::: footer-nav
[← Back to Overview](index.html)[Continue to Subsystems →](subsystems.html)
:::
